title: Cookbook of CI-rendered worked examples
short_description: Write the complete cookbook — a map scene, a character dialogue scene, and a full 2-minute episode — all rendered in CI at low res with verify reports snapshot-tested, structured for agent pattern-matching.
long_description_md:
# Cookbook of CI-rendered worked examples

## Why this exists

Product-spec capability 11 names the cookbook explicitly: "a 3-beat map scene, a character dialogue scene, a full 2-minute episode." These are not marketing samples — they are the pattern-matching substrate an agent reads before writing its own scene. Technical-architecture testing strategy: "cookbook examples render fully in CI (low res) and their verify reports are snapshot-tested." If a cookbook example ever silently stops verifying or its render breaks, agents copying its pattern inherit the breakage — so this is a CI-enforced contract, not static prose.

## Roadmap fit

Phase 7, second done-when bullet, verbatim: "Cookbook complete: map scene, character scene, full 2-minute episode — all CI-rendered, verify-snapshot-tested." Depends on `map-mini-episode` (Phase 4 — the map node vocabulary and camera-flight patterns this cookbook's map example reuses), `pack-pluggability-proof` (Phase 5 — proves the same project renders under both packs with zero scene-code change, which the cookbook demonstrates concretely), `subtitle-rendering` (Phase 2 — burned-in subtitle typography the dialogue and episode examples use).

Sibling `agent-docs-surface` writes SKILL.md/llms.txt/AGENTS.md/verification-rule catalog and links to this cookbook — it does not build cookbook content. This task builds the examples; it does not write the onboarding prose that points at them.

## Required scope

Three complete worked examples under `examples/cookbook/`:

1. **`map_scene/`** — BaseMap, ≥2 Territory fills, Border, InvasionArrow (curved growth), MapLabel, map camera flight, beat-annotated `script.md` (per capability 11, ~3 beats minimum).
2. **`character_dialogue/`** — ≥2 Character nodes with pose changes, entrance/exit animation families, burned-in subtitles timed to beats.
3. **`full_episode/`** — combines map + character + subtitle patterns into one coherent ~2-minute (~120s) narration-driven project — the composite pattern an agent scales toward the eventual 15-20 min flagship.

Each example ships `project.py`, `script.md`, and a committed verify-report JSON snapshot fixture. CI renders each at low resolution (e.g. 480p) via `anima render --resolution 480p`/`--json` and re-runs `anima verify --json`, comparing the fresh verify output against the committed snapshot (comparison against a committed baseline, never generate-and-compare-against-itself in the same run). A cookbook index (`examples/cookbook/README.md`) lists each example with the node types and animation families it demonstrates, for agent pattern-matching — cross-checked against the examples' actual `anima inspect --json` output so the index never drifts from what the code actually contains.

## Out of scope

- SKILL.md, llms.txt, AGENTS.md, verification-rule catalog — `agent-docs-surface`.
- Running a live fresh-agent session against the cookbook — `fresh-agent-test`.
- Full-resolution or full-length (15-20 min) rendering — that's the flagship, `v1-flagship-episode`.
- Any new engine feature, node type, or verify rule — cookbook uses only what already shipped by Phase 6.
roadmap_phase: Phase 7: Agent onboarding surface + the v1 bar

deliverables.description: examples/cookbook/map_scene/ — BaseMap, ≥2 Territory fills, Border, curved-growth InvasionArrow, MapLabel, camera flight, ≥3-beat script.md. CI renders at 480p and verify JSON compared against committed snapshot fixture.

examples/cookbook/character_dialogue/ — ≥2 Character nodes with pose changes, entrance/exit families, burned-in subtitles timed to beats. CI renders at 480p and verify JSON compared against committed snapshot fixture.

examples/cookbook/full_episode/ — ~120s composite of map + character + subtitle patterns, beat-timing sums to ~120s (asserted), rendered at 480p, verify JSON compared against committed snapshot fixture.

examples/cookbook/README.md index listing all 3 examples and the node types/animation families each demonstrates, cross-checked against each example's real `anima inspect --json` output so the index can't drift from actual code.
